title: Today's AI Brief: Cisco and OpenAI redefine enterprise engineering with Codex
summary: Cisco and OpenAI team up with Codex, Meta develops an AI pendant, and the evolving landscape of AI in enterprise and consumer tech.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI brief. Big news in the world of artificial intelligence today. I'm looking at a couple of developments that caught my eye - Cisco and OpenAI joining forces with Codex, and Meta reportedly working on an AI-powered pendant. Let's dive in.

First up, Cisco and OpenAI are redefining enterprise engineering with Codex. This partnership aims to help Cisco scale AI-native development, speed up AI defense work, and automate defect remediation. What stands out to me here is how Codex is being used to make AI more practical and applicable in a business setting. It's not just about the tech; it's about making it work for real-world problems. For instance, automating defect remediation can save companies a significant amount of time and resources, allowing them to focus on more strategic initiatives.

Next, I've got my eye on Meta, which is reportedly developing an AI pendant. Now, this sounds like something straight out of a sci-fi movie, but apparently, it's real. The idea here is to create a wearable AI device that can perform various tasks. I'm intrigued by how this could change the way we interact with AI in our daily lives. Imagine having a personal assistant that's always with you, ready to help with whatever you need. It's an exciting prospect, and I'm curious to see how it develops.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
